claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 6 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 6 (under 16)
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£0.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child in hospital for 13 weeks but claimant regularly spends on child
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£0.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
circumstances: Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£0.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
Child is not in local authority care
Child in hospital for 13 weeks but claimant regularly spends on child
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for UK Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's begin the Child Benefit eligibility assessment.

**First, I need to know about the child (or children) you're claiming for.**

**How old is your child?**

Please let me know which category applies:
- **Under 16 years old**
- **16 to 19 years old**
- **20 years old or over**

If you have multiple children, please tell me each of their ages and I'll assess eligibility for each one.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi, thanks for helping me with this. I'm asking about my daughter Alex - she's 6 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! Let me continue the assessment for Alex, your 6-year-old daughter.

Since Alex is under 16 years old, she automatically qualifies based on age. Let me navigate to the next step.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check about Alex's work status.

**Is Alex working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work at all)?**

Given that Alex is only 6 years old, I assume she's not working. Can you confirm this is correct?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's right, she doesn't work at all - she's only 6!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Of course! That makes perfect sense. Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Is Alex NOT receiving any benefits in her own right?**

This means she's not personally claiming benefits like Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit. 

Again, at 6 years old, this would be very unusual - can you confirm that Alex is not receiving any such benefits in her own name?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, she doesn't get any benefits in her own name.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Is Alex currently in an apprenticeship?**

At 6 years old, I'm confident the answer is no, but I need to confirm - Alex is not in any kind of apprenticeship programme, correct?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, of course not - she's only 6!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Absolutely, just checking all the boxes! Let me continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check about your residency status.

**Do you meet UK residency requirements?**

You can qualify through **any one** of the following:

1. **You normally live in the UK**
2. **You have settled status** under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. **You have pre-settled status** and meet one of these conditions:
   - You're working and earning above the National Insurance threshold
   - You're actively seeking work (can claim for up to 91 days)
   - You have sufficient financial resources and aren't claiming certain benefits
   - You're studying and have sufficient financial resources
   - You're a family member of an EEA or Swiss national with the right to reside
4. **You're a Crown servant** living abroad (or in certain qualifying countries)

Which of these applies to you? For most people, the first option (normally living in the UK) is the relevant one.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, thank you! Living in the UK means you meet the residency requirements. Let me continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Are you responsible for Alex?**

You can qualify through **any one** of the following:

1. **You live with Alex**
2. **You contribute to Alex's upkeep** – this means paying at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or equivalent in kind) towards her care. This can include food, clothes, pocket money, housing, or presents.
3. **You pay maintenance** for Alex under a court order or agreement

Which of these applies to you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: She doesn't live with me, and I'm not currently contributing financially to her upkeep.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I understand. Based on what you've told me, I need to ask a clarifying question.

You've mentioned that Alex:
- Doesn't live with you
- You're not currently contributing financially to her upkeep

Unfortunately, to be eligible for Child Benefit, you need to be **responsible** for the child in one of these ways:

1. Living with the child, **OR**
2. Contributing to their upkeep (at least the equivalent of the Child Benefit amount), **OR**
3. Paying maintenance under a court order or agreement

Since neither of the first two apply, can I just confirm: **Are you paying any maintenance for Alex under a court order or formal agreement?**

If not, I'm afraid you may not meet the responsibility requirements for claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not paying any maintenance under a court order or agreement.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for confirming that. I'm sorry, but based on the information you've provided, I need to let you know the outcome.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Child Benefit Eligibility Result for Alex

**Result: NOT ELIGIBLE** ❌

I'm sorry to tell you that based on the information you've provided, you are **not currently eligible** to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

### Why?

While Alex qualifies as an eligible child (she's under 16, not working, not receiving benefits, and not in an apprenticeship), you don't meet the **responsibility requirement** because you:

- Don't live with Alex
- Aren't contributing financially to her upkeep
- Aren't paying maintenance under a court order or agreement

### What does "responsible" mean for Child Benefit?

To claim Child Benefit, you must be responsible for the child in one of these ways:
1. **Living with them**, OR
2. **Contributing to their upkeep** – paying at least the Child Benefit amount (or equivalent in kind) towards their care, such as food, clothes, pocket money, housing, or presents, OR
3. **Paying maintenance** under a court order or formal agreement

### What can you do?

- **If your circumstances change** (e.g., Alex comes to live with you, or you start contributing to her upkeep), you may become eligible
- **The person who Alex lives with** or who is financially responsible for her may be able to claim Child Benefit for her instead

If you have any questions or if your situation changes, please feel free to ask. I'm here to help.

reasonings: Alex is 6 years old and meets the age requirement (under 16). She is not working, not receiving benefits in her own right, and is not in an apprenticeship - so she qualifies as a child for Child Benefit purposes. However, the claimant does not meet the 'responsibility' requirement because they: (1) do not live with Alex, (2) are not contributing financially to her upkeep, and (3) are not paying maintenance under a court order or agreement. To claim Child Benefit, you must be responsible for the child through at least one of these means.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex because they do not meet the responsibility requirement. While Alex qualifies as an eligible child (under 16, not working, not receiving benefits, not in an apprenticeship), the claimant must either live with the child or contribute to their upkeep to claim Child Benefit.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

Amount equals or exceeds Child Benefit payment

She is not working, not receiving benefits in her own right, and is not in an apprenticeship - so she qualifies as a child for Child Benefit purposes. To claim Child Benefit, you must be responsible for the child through at least one of these means.
